name: Vehicle Acceleration
description: A vehicle converts driver throttle input into forward motion through engine, traction, surface, and authority rules.
design_notes: Acceleration feel depends on more than a velocity cap. The design should define throttle_input sampling, acceleration_curve_id, torque response, traction limits, surface modifiers, fixed-step integration, and whether the driver or server owns input authority.
design_purpose: Make vehicles feel responsive while preserving weight, traction, and readable handling.
Give designers control over launch feel, mid-pace pull, hill behavior, and surface differences.
Support upgrades, fuel, damage, and boost systems that modify vehicle movement coherently.
player_fantasy: The player feels the vehicle surge forward under their control and learns its power curve.

parameters.name: vehicle_id
parameters.description: Stable vehicle identifier used for input ownership, physics state, persistence, and upgrades.

parameters.name: driver_player_id
parameters.description: Player or AI currently authorized to provide throttle and steering input.

parameters.name: throttle_input
parameters.description: Normalized driver acceleration input captured for the current simulation tick.

parameters.name: acceleration_curve_id
parameters.description: Curve mapping throttle and current velocity into drive acceleration.

parameters.name: traction_coefficient
parameters.description: Surface-adjusted grip multiplier that limits how much drive can become motion.

parameters.name: velocity_cap_kph
parameters.description: Upper forward velocity target used by drivetrain and upgrade balancing.

edge_cases: Throttle is held while the vehicle is airborne, upside down, or missing wheel contact.
Surface changes from asphalt to mud during the same fixed step that acceleration is applied.
Client prediction applies throttle but the server rejects driver ownership after a seat swap.
A vehicle loads on a slope and starts moving before input authority has been restored.
common_bugs: Acceleration is integrated from render delta and changes with frame rate.
Throttle input remains latched after driver disconnect, pause, or seat exit.
Traction modifiers are applied after velocity cap, making mud affect launch but not top pace.
Network correction restores transform but not wheel contact or drivetrain state.
balancing_notes: Strong launch acceleration feels responsive but can make tight tracks or combat encounters hard to read.
Surface traction should change route choices without making vehicles feel randomly underpowered.
Upgrade modifiers should affect curves predictably so players can compare parts before installing them.
accessibility_notes: Offer throttle assist, input smoothing, or digital trigger support for players without analog controls.
Provide camera options that reduce sudden pitch or acceleration shake.

implementation_notes.unity: Read throttle_input from the active driver, queue it with a client_input_sequence, and apply drivetrain acceleration in FixedUpdate. Rigidbody or WheelCollider state should include traction_coefficient, surface id, velocity, and authority_owner_id for rollback snapshots.
implementation_notes.godot: Use _physics_process for vehicle integration and keep input state separate from rendered vehicle nodes. VehicleBody3D or custom controllers should sample ground_surface_id before applying acceleration_curve_id so tire behavior and UI telemetry agree.
implementation_notes.unreal: Chaos Vehicles or custom movement components should receive compressed throttle input from the owning PlayerController, while the server validates driver_player_id and seat ownership. Replicate movement state with correction for wheel contact and drivetrain variables.
implementation_notes.web: For Phaser, Three.js, or custom physics, run vehicle acceleration in a fixed-step loop with stored input frames. Browser clients can predict throttle response, but authoritative sessions should reconcile vehicle pose, velocity, and surface state from server ticks.

example_games.title: Mario Kart 8
example_games.note: Uses readable acceleration and traction differences across kart setups.

example_games.title: Forza Horizon 5
example_games.note: Uses vehicle acceleration curves as part of car handling and tuning.
